findings.Problem: Reason-only guard skips validation and silently falls through: `cmd.Flags().Changed("status")` does not guard --reason, so `--reason "text" --resolve` is required but `--reason "text"` alone bypasses the guard and proceeds to markDebtResolved which then errors on missing id (user gets an unhelpful "no open item" error instead of a clear usage hint). The --status case correctly uses `Changed("status")`. Align --reason guard with the --status pattern: `cmd.Flags().Changed("status")

The error message for invalid `--status` value uses `mustFlag(cmd, "status")` (original casing) while the lookup uses the lowercased `status` variable, so a user typing "--status RESOLVED" sees "invalid --status 'RESOLVED'" rather than the canonical lowercase form. Not a crash, but inconsistent with the lowercasing that actually controls the logic.
findings.Fix: Use the already-lowercased `status` variable in the error message: fmt.Errorf("invalid --status %q: expected resolved

findings.Problem: The status validation rejects empty string, but no test covers `--status ""`.
findings.Fix: Add a test with an empty --status value and assert a usage error.
findings.Evidence: The validation `!resolveStatuses[status]` will reject empty string; only "bogus" is tested.

findings.Problem: The reason trimming logic treats whitespace-only reason as empty (preserving existing justification), but no test covers `--reason "   "`.
findings.Fix: Add a test with whitespace-only --reason and verify that an existing justification is preserved.
findings.Evidence: Code uses `strings.TrimSpace(reason)`; whitespace-only yields empty string, which falls through to preserve existing.

findings.Problem: The new tests (`TestDebtResolve_WontfixStatusFoldsItemOutOfOpenList`, `TestDebtResolve_MarkWontfixSetsStatusAndFoldsOut`, etc.) cover the happy path for wontfix but do not include a test for the idempotency case: resolving an already-closed item with `--status wontfix` when a `resolved` record already exists (or vice versa). The existing `TestDebtResolve_MarkResolvedIsIdempotent` covers this for `--status resolved`, but the wontfix variant is missing, leaving the "already resolved" hardcoded-message regression (finding 1) untested for wontfix closures.
findings.Fix: Add `TestDebtResolve_MarkWontfixIsIdempotentAgainstResolved` (and symmetric) to mirror the existing resolved idempotency test
findings.Evidence: TestDebtResolve_MarkResolvedIsIdempotent covers resolved; wontfix counterpart absent

findings.Problem: `TestDebtResolve_NoReasonPreservesExistingJustification` verifies that omitting `--reason` preserves an existing justification, but does not test the edge case where the original record has an empty `Justification` and `--reason` is also omitted — does the new record get a blank `Justification` (zero-value field) or does it remain unset? This matters for clients that distinguish between "no justification provided" (nil/empty) and "justification is empty string".
findings.Fix: Add assertion that `terminal.Justification == ""` (not uninitialized) when no `--reason` is supplied and original had no justification
findings.Evidence: assert.Equal(t, "original enrichment note", terminal.Justification, ...) // empty-string case not covered
